question: Find customers who have not placed any order
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.name, c.email FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.order_id IS NULL;
notes: Anti-join pattern; may return empty on seed data

question: Show customer lifetime value distribution by country
ground_truth_sql: SELECT country, COUNT(*) AS customer_count, AVG(lifetime_value) AS avg_ltv, MIN(lifetime_value) AS min_ltv, MAX(lifetime_value) AS max_ltv FROM customers GROUP BY country ORDER BY avg_ltv DESC;
notes: Multi-aggregate GROUP BY on single table

question: Identify VIP customers in the top 10 percent by lifetime value
ground_truth_sql: SELECT name, email, lifetime_value FROM customers WHERE lifetime_value >= (SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.9) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY lifetime_value) FROM customers) ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;
notes: PERCENTILE_CONT subquery; valid even if all customers qualify on small dataset

question: Show the top 5 customers by total amount spent
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.name, c.email, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_spend FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name, c.email ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 5;

question: Find customers who haven't placed an order in the last 90 days
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.name, c.email, MAX(o.order_date) AS last_order_date FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name, c.email HAVING MAX(o.order_date) < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' OR MAX(o.order_date) IS NULL;
notes: INTERVAL date comparison in HAVING; may return all customers on fresh DB

question: How many new customers joined each month?
ground_truth_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) AS month, COUNT(*) AS new_customers FROM customers GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) ORDER BY month;

question: Show the average order value for each customer
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count, AVG(o.total_amount) AS avg_order_value FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name ORDER BY avg_order_value DESC;

question: Find customers who have both completed and pending orders
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.name, c.email FROM customers c WHERE c.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed') AND c.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending');
notes: Two subqueries in WHERE with AND; valid even if empty result

question: Which customer placed the highest value single order?
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.name, o.order_id, o.total_amount FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id ORDER BY o.total_amount DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Show customers who have left at least one review
ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT c.name, c.email, COUNT(r.review_id) AS review_count FROM customers c JOIN reviews r ON c.customer_id = r.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name, c.email ORDER BY review_count DESC;
notes: JOIN + GROUP BY HAVING implied by JOIN existence
